target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `1bda9a56c450bbdb03b7fbbcd670b5d7f5cf1bac`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.hSSPqV/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.hSSPqV/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "1bda9a56c450bbdb03b7fbbcd670b5d7f5cf1bac",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix: harden config schema extension id maps [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: generated config schemas wrote plugin and channel ids directly into JSON schema `properties` maps.
- Solution: normalize those dynamic schema property maps to null-prototype records and add entries with safe data-property semantics.
- What changed: added schema-map helpers for plugin/channel config schema assembly and a regression test for object-special extension ids.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): extension id normalization, config defaults, lookup path restrictions, and ordinary plugin/channel schema output shape are unchanged.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Motivation

- Generated config schema output should stay structurally stable when extension metadata contains object-special key names.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- N/A in public PR metadata.
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `buildConfigSchema` safely represents plugin and channel ids that match object-special key names as own schema entries.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout using the repository Vitest wrapper for the runtime-config test project.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 45 passed (45)` for `src/config/schema.test.ts`.
- Observed result after fix: generated plugin and channel schema property maps keep a null prototype and expose `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` as own entries with reachable schema properties.
- What was not tested: full test suite, packaged build, and a live gateway run with an installed extension using those ids.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the focused regression failed before the final normalization pass because one generated plugin property map did not keep the expected null prototype.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: dynamic extension ids were inserted into ordinary JSON schema `properties` objects with bracket assignment.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression coverage for extension ids that overlap object-special key names.
- Contributing context (if known): schema merge cloning can turn a previously normalized property map back into an ordinary object, so final schema assembly also needs normalization.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: plugin and channel ids `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype` remain own entries in generated schema property maps without changing those maps' prototypes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `buildConfigSchema` owns the property-map generation path, so a direct schema test covers both plugin and channel insertion without unrelated runtime setup.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Generated config schema output now safely includes plugin/channel ids matching object-special key names as own properties. No config defaults or migrations changed.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local worktree
- Runtime/container: repository Vitest wrapper in local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): generated plugin/channel config schema metadata
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Generate a config schema with plugin and channel ids named `__proto__`, `constructor`, and `prototype`.
2. Inspect the generated plugin and channel schema `properties` maps.
3. Run `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts`.

### Expected

- Dynamic schema maps keep inert prototypes and store each special id as an own schema property.

### Actual

- The focused config schema test passed with all 45 tests green.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused config schema test passed after the change; regression checks plugin and channel ids for all three object-special names.
- Edge cases checked: own-property presence, null-prototype maps, and reachable nested plugin/channel config schema properties.
- What you did **not** verify: full suite, packaging, live gateway startup, or installed third-party extension behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] N/A; no bot review conversations were handled in this metadata draft.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: internal code that expects these generated `properties` maps to inherit `Object.prototype` could behave differently.
  - Mitigation: schema traversal in the touched path uses static object helpers and enumerable entries; JSON serialization remains unchanged for consumers that receive the schema over the wire.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by suppressing partial streaming when `canCaptionFinalTts` is true (`shouldSuppressPartialForTts`).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression, plain tagged block delivery |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 200 passed (200)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 65 passed (65)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 277 tests passed across 4 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode sending voice without caption or producing "No response generated" errors.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `9f392339`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch via `npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.1.tgz`
  2. Restarted Gateway
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent messages triggering `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directives
  4. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` — sent plain messages

- **Evidence after fix — Tagged mode (`messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`)**:

  ![Tagged mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/754E69B76FD144D9AFA90683C057AF30.jpg)

  Tagged directive-only replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No separate text message, no "No response generated" error.

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`)**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/3469AF0AD1A34379A1ED2F773D1CB35D.jpg)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn.

- **Observed result after fix**: Both tagged and always TTS auto modes deliver voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no "No response generated" errors.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested).

Closes #83511

target.title: feat(embedded-runner): expose prep stage timings
target.body:
## Summary
- Expose embedded runner prep stage snapshots on `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult`.
- Include the same prep stage summary on the `agent_end` hook event for plugin observability.
- Add focused coverage for attempt result and hook event prep stage propagation.
- Document the new optional hook field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has May embedded-runner/subagent performance work and trace logging for startup stages. It still does not expose the attempt prep stage summary to `agent_end` hook observers. This PR is narrowed to that missing diagnostics surface so plugins can collect stage timing evidence without patching core locally.

## Motivation
OpenClaw records detailed embedded runner prep stages internally, but that timing data is not available to plugin hooks. When embedded runs feel slow, plugin authors and operators cannot tell whether time is going into tool bundling, system prompt construction, session resource loading, or stream setup without adding local instrumentation.

Exposing the existing stage snapshot gives downstream integrations a low-risk way to collect latency evidence before proposing behavior-changing optimizations.

## Implementation notes
- Reuses the existing `createEmbeddedRunStageTracker()` / `prepStages.snapshot()` path.
- Keeps all new fields optional.
- Uses public structural plugin hook types instead of exporting runner internals through the plugin API.
- Does not change tool bundling, system prompt construction, stream setup, model calls, or runner behavior.
- Documents `agent_end.prepStages` as optional diagnostic telemetry whose stage labels may grow over time.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded runner prep stage timings are visible to a real non-bundled plugin's `agent_end` hook without changing the embedded run outcome.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `c76f0a14da307ab627248825143f312ca7b1b25c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, temporary local plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths` with `hooks.allowConversationAccess=true`, `node openclaw.mjs agent --local`, and the repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebased onto current `origin/main` `d4f6e0a1f24a047cf64afec3437847d4797a4c92`, regenerated the Plugin SDK API baseline, ran focused tests/checks, then created a temporary plugin that writes its `agent_end` event's `prepStages` to JSONL. Started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs`, ran the local CLI against the temporary config, and asserted both the CLI result and the plugin hook output.

Evidence after fix:

```text

exit 0

exit 0

exit 0

git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main HEAD
exit 0
```

Current-head live CLI/plugin assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "c76f0a14da307ab627248825143f312ca7b1b25c",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK",
  "hookSuccess": true,
  "hookHasPrepStages": true,
  "hookTotalMs": 115,
  "hookStageNames": [
    "workspace-sandbox",
    "skills",
    "core-plugin-tools",
    "bootstrap-context",
    "bundle-tools",
    "system-prompt",
    "session-resource-loader",
    "agent-session",
    "stream-setup"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: A standalone embedded `openclaw agent --local` run completed successfully against the mock OpenAI server, returned `OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, and the temporary plugin's real `agent_end` handler observed `event.prepStages` with the expected prep stages. The current head also verifies `runEmbeddedAgent` returns `result.meta.prepStages` from the attempt prep-stage summary.

What was not tested: A live paid external model/provider run was not run. The proof used OpenClaw's deterministic local OpenAI-compatible response server to avoid provider latency/cost while still exercising the real embedded runner, local CLI, plugin loader, and `agent_end` hook dispatch.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- Risk: plugin authors might treat stage names as permanent.
- Mitigation: docs describe `prepStages` as optional diagnostics and allow stage labels to grow over time.

target.title: refactor(whatsapp): introduce inbound message contexts
target.body:
## Summary

This PR reorganizes WhatsApp `WebInboundMessage` so inbound callback data is grouped by what the data represents instead of being exposed only as one large flat object.

The new shape separates inbound message facts into five explicit contexts:

- `event`: message id, timestamp, and batching metadata
- `payload`: message body, media, location, and structured payload context
- `quote`: quoted/replied-to message context
- `group`: group subject, participants, and mention metadata
- `platform`: WhatsApp transport details and actions such as reply, media send, and typing

Downstream WhatsApp auto-reply, monitor, identity, group gating, reaction, reply delivery, media, quote, and test-support code now reads these facts from the new contexts. The shipped flat callback fields and methods remain available as deprecated aliases so existing callback consumers can upgrade without a breaking shape change.

## API Shape

Code that receives a WhatsApp inbound message should prefer the relevant context, for example `msg.payload.body`, `msg.event.id`, `msg.group?.mentions`, `msg.quote?.body`, and `msg.platform.reply(...)`.

For compatibility, existing flat callback fields and methods such as `msg.body`, `msg.chatId`, `msg.reply(...)`, and `msg.mediaPath` are still populated and marked deprecated in the exported `WebInboundMessage` type.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Live Scenario Validation

All recorded live scenarios are currently PASS.

| Scenario | Status | What was live-tested |
|---:|---|---|
| 01 | PASS | Public API exposes canonical nested inbound contexts and deprecated flat aliases. |
| 02 | PASS | Compatibility docs and registry describe the inbound context migration. |
| 03 | PASS | Normalizer accepts canonical/legacy input and rejects partial nested input. |
| 04 | PASS | Live `monitorWebInbox` direct text callback exposed nested contexts and aliases. |
| 05 | PASS | Direct DM auto-reply still replies after nested context migration. |
| 06 | PASS | Direct allowlist admits the configured sender. |
| 07 | PASS | Direct allowlist rejects an unconfigured sender. |
| 08 | PASS | Direct media inbound exposes `payload.media` and remains replyable. |
| 09 | PASS | Native WhatsApp location share exposed `payload.location`. |
| 10 | PASS | Native contact/vCard share exposed `payload.untrustedStructuredContext`. |
| 11 | PASS | Native quoted direct DM exposed canonical quote context and deprecated reply aliases. |
| 12 | PASS | Direct debounce batches rapid text messages with nested batch metadata. |
| 13 | PASS | Debounce exclusions behaved correctly. |
| 14 | PASS | `replyToMode: "first"` quotes the first inbound message. |
| 15 | PASS | Batched reply quote uses the selected source message id. |
| 16 | PASS | Group allowlist admits a configured group. |
| 17 | PASS | Group allowlist drops an unconfigured group. |
| 18 | PASS | Group sender allowlist admits the configured work sender. |
| 19 | PASS | Group sender allowlist blocks a non-allowed sender. |
| 20 | PASS | Explicit group mention triggers reply and sets mention context. |
| 21 | PASS | Unmentioned group message stores pending history and does not reply. |
| 22 | PASS | Later group mention injects pending group history. |
| 23 | PASS | Reply-to-bot in group acts as an implicit mention. |
| 24 | PASS | Group metadata context includes subject and participants when available. |
| 25 | PASS | Group context visibility filters pending history and quotes by sender policy. |
| 26 | PASS | Direct voice preflight preserves access control, ack timing, and transcript context. |
| 27 | PASS | Group voice transcript can satisfy mention gating after group policy passes. |
| 28 | PASS | Rejected group voice note does not transcribe or send feedback. |
| 29 | PASS | Broadcast group fanout routes configured agents with per-agent ack behavior. |
| 30 | PASS | Broadcast voice note shares one transcript while preserving per-agent route behavior. |
| 31 | PASS | Ack reactions target the exact inbound message ids and respect group mention state. |
| 32 | PASS | `reactionLevel: "off"` suppresses ack and status reactions. |
| 33 | PASS | Status reaction lifecycle handles visible delivery, silent handled hooks, and backend error delivery. |
| 34 | PASS | Text reply delivery uses `platform.reply`. |
| 35 | PASS | Media reply delivery uses `platform.sendMedia`. |
| 36 | PASS | `message_received` hook emits when enabled and is suppressed when disabled. |
| 37 | PASS | Multi-account override isolates policy, reply, reaction, debounce, and media config. |
| 38 | PASS | Direct peer identity and last-route updates use normalized nested sender facts. |
| 39 | PASS | Local media shape preserves media facts; remote-only URL variant is supported downstream but not producible by the live WhatsApp monitor path. |

## Review Note

The inbound callback contract now exposes the nested context shape while preserving the shipped flat callback aliases. The runtime only creates optional contexts when their facts exist, so non-media messages do not expose a truthy `payload.media` and direct debounced batches do not expose a truthy `group` context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp inbound callback messages now expose event, payload, quote, group, and platform contexts while preserving deprecated flat aliases for the shipped callback contract.

Real environment tested: Local focused WhatsApp extension tests plus live WhatsApp scenario validation against real OpenClaw gateway/runtime and WhatsApp accounts.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final patch, and the live scenarios summarized below were exercised against real WhatsApp/OpenClaw runtime behavior.

Evidence after fix: Focused WhatsApp inbox and media tests passed, whitespace diff check passed, autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings, and all 39 recorded live scenarios are PASS.

Observed result after fix: Inbound monitor and auto-reply paths consume the nested message contexts for body, media, location, contact, quote, group, event, platform reply, media send, typing behavior, reactions, routing, hooks, and account overrides, while fallback flat aliases remain populated.

What was not tested: Broad Testbox/CI lanes. A few live variants required manual native WhatsApp input or source-runtime validation where the packaged/full-build path was impractical on this machine.

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: fix(agents): keep migrated session entry ids unique on v1 upgrade
target.body:
## Summary

`migrateV1ToV2` (`src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts`) assigns every migrated entry a fresh id via `generateId(ids)`, where `ids` is meant to be the running set of already-assigned ids so the generator can avoid collisions (`generateId` loops `randomUUID().slice(0, 8)` and returns the first value not in the set). But the loop never adds the freshly generated id back into `ids`, so the set stays empty for the entire migration and the collision check is a no-op.

On a v1 to v2 upgrade two entries can therefore be minted with the same 8-hex id. The migration also rebuilds the parent/child tree from those ids (`entry.parentId = prevId; prevId = entry.id`), so a collision makes the second entry adopt the colliding id as both its own id and its `parentId`, leaving an entry parented to itself and a duplicate node in the rebuilt branch.

Root cause: missing `ids.add(entry.id)` after generation. Fix: add the generated id to the running set so subsequent `generateId(ids)` calls actually see it and retry on collision.

Why this is not one-sided (sibling surfaces): `generateId` has one other accumulating caller, `createBranchedSession`, which rebuilds its dedup set on every call from `pathEntryIds` plus the already-written `labelEntries`, so it already feeds the generator the prior ids and is unaffected. `createSessionId` uses `uuidv7()` and is unrelated. `migrateV1ToV2` is the only caller that holds a single running set across a loop and failed to populate it, so the repair is scoped to that one spot.

Impact is low in practice (only v1-only sessions being upgraded, and only when two independent `randomUUID().slice(0, 8)` draws collide within one file, an 8-hex birthday chance), so this is a small correctness fix rather than a user-facing regression.

## Verification

Local (Node 22.19.0, pnpm 11.2.2):

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/session-migrate-id-dedup.test.ts` - 1/1 pass (new regression test).
- Red-proof: reverting only the `ids.add(entry.id)` line makes the new test fail with `AssertionError: expected 1 to be 2` (both migrated entries collapse to one id); with the fix it passes.
- Type-aware `oxlint` via `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json` on both files - exit 0, clean.
- `npx oxfmt --check` on both files - clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - exit 0.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** a v1 to v2 session upgrade no longer mints duplicate entry ids (and the self-parented branch that follows) when the id generator's first draw collides.
- **Real environment tested:** the real production migration path in this checkout, `SessionManager.open(file)` -> `loadEntriesFromFile` -> `migrateToCurrentVersion` -> `migrateV1ToV2` -> `rewriteFile`, exercised end to end against an on-disk v1 `.jsonl` file. Only `node:crypto`'s `randomUUID` is stubbed, and only to make the otherwise-probabilistic 8-hex collision deterministic; the migration, tree rebuild, and file rewrite are the real code.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** wrote a v1 session file (a `version: 1` header plus two id-less message entries), forced `randomUUID` to return the same first-8 hex twice and then a distinct value, opened the file through `SessionManager.open`, and inspected the migrated entries' ids and parentIds; then reverted the one-line fix and re-ran.

Behavioral before/after (real `SessionManager.open` migration path; only the `randomUUID` draws are stubbed):

```
# fix reverted -> both migrated entries get the same id
migrated message ids: ["deadbeef", "deadbeef"]   # Set size 1, expected 2 -> AssertionError: expected 1 to be 2
entry[1].parentId === entry[1].id                 # self-parented / duplicate node

# fix applied -> generator retries on the seeded collision
migrated message ids: ["deadbeef", "cafef00d"]   # unique
entry[1].parentId === entry[0].id                 # correct chain
```

- **Evidence after fix:** with the collision seeded, the migrated file has two distinct entry ids and `entry[1].parentId` points at `entry[0]`; reverting the fix yields one shared id and a self-referential `parentId`.
- **Observed result after fix:** v1 migration produces a clean, uniquely-keyed entry tree even under a forced id collision.
- **What was not tested:** a natural (un-stubbed) collision, which is an 8-hex birthday event and not reproducible on demand without controlling the RNG. The stub only removes that randomness; it does not change the code under test.

target.body:
## Summary

- Group the sidebar's existing five most recent sessions by resolved channel.
- Resolve the channel from session row metadata first, then fall back to session key parsing for channel-backed sessions, including account-scoped direct session keys.
- Add sidebar group styling and regression coverage for the top-five-before-grouping behavior.

## Why

The sidebar recent list is used as a quick navigation surface. Grouping only the already-selected five most recent sessions keeps it compact while making recent conversations easier to scan by source, without trying to surface every channel.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts"`
- `git diff --check -- ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/types.ts ui/src/styles/layout.css ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: the sidebar recent list now groups the already-filtered top five sessions by channel, including account-scoped direct keys such as `agent:main:feishu:main:direct:<peer>`.
Real environment tested: Tencent Cloud OpenClaw Gateway `http://106.54.23.243:18789` running `openclaw@2026.5.31-beta.3`, connected from this PR branch's local patched Control UI dev server at `http://127.0.0.1:5173/sessions`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: temporarily added `http://127.0.0.1:5173` to `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins`, restarted the Tencent Cloud gateway, opened the local patched Control UI with the Tencent Cloud Gateway URL, confirmed the Gateway URL prompt, waited for the Sessions view to load, and captured the rendered sidebar.
Evidence after fix: screenshots captured from the real Tencent Cloud Gateway session list at local artifacts `artifacts/sidebar-recent-channel-groups-proof.png` and `artifacts/sidebar-recent-channel-groups-proof-after-parser-fix.png`; copied live browser automation output from the parser-fix run:

```text
REAL_OPENCLAW_SETUP=http://106.54.23.243:18789 gateway + local patched UI http://127.0.0.1:5173/sessions
SIDEBAR_RECENT_GROUPS=[{"channel":"feishu","label":"Feishu","sessions":["饶宇"]},{"channel":"openclaw-weixin","label":"Openclaw Weixin","sessions":["o9cq80zwIKGaDsfYCG0zOli4OSQw@im.wechat"]}]
```

Observed result after fix: the sidebar Recent section rendered grouped channel headings for the real top recent sessions; account-scoped Feishu direct sessions resolved under Feishu instead of Other.
What was not tested: production deployment of this branch's bundled Control UI assets was not installed on the Tencent Cloud host; the proof used the local patched UI dev server connected to the real Tencent Cloud Gateway data.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by suppressing partial streaming when `canCaptionFinalTts` is true (`shouldSuppressPartialForTts`).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression, plain tagged block delivery |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 200 passed (200)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 65 passed (65)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 277 tests passed across 4 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode sending voice without caption or producing "No response generated" errors.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `9f392339`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch via `npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.1.tgz`
  2. Restarted Gateway
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent messages triggering `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directives
  4. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` — sent plain messages

- **Evidence after fix — Tagged mode (`messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`)**:

  ![Tagged mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/754E69B76FD144D9AFA90683C057AF30.jpg)

  Tagged directive-only replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No separate text message, no "No response generated" error.

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`)**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/3469AF0AD1A34379A1ED2F773D1CB35D.jpg)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn.

- **Observed result after fix**: Both tagged and always TTS auto modes deliver voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no "No response generated" errors.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested).

Closes #83511

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `1bda9a56c450bbdb03b7fbbcd670b5d7f5cf1bac`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.hSSPqV/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.hSSPqV/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "1bda9a56c450bbdb03b7fbbcd670b5d7f5cf1bac",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(hooks): reject internal session namespaces
target.body:
## Summary
- reject hook-provided session keys that target internal `subagent:`, `acp:`, or `cron:` namespaces
- validate `hooks.defaultSessionKey` against the same reserved namespaces at config load time
- add unit + server hook regressions covering request, mapping, and config paths

## Why
Hook agent runs execute through the isolated cron path. If external hooks can choose an existing internal session namespace, the resulting run inherits session-key scoped control state intended only for internal subagent / ACP / cron flows.

## Testing
- pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key.test.ts

target.title: fix(tui): block unsupported local slash commands
target.body:
## Summary
- Local embedded TUI bypasses the gateway `chat.send` command-recovery path (`EmbeddedTuiBackend.sendChat` runs `agentCommandFromIngress` directly), so every advertised shared slash command that the TUI has no local handler for would fall through to `sendMessage(raw)` and become a model/user turn.
- Block **all** advertised-but-unimplemented shared commands in local mode (not just `/status` and `/compact`): `handleCommand`'s `default` branch now answers them with an explicit unsupported-mode system response instead of sending them to the model.
- Stop advertising those commands in local mode: `getSlashCommands({ local: true })` no longer appends unsupported shared commands/aliases to autocomplete, and `helpText({ local: true })` drops `/commands` and `/status`.
- Locally-supported routed commands (`/context`, `/goal`, `/stop`, `/btw`, `/side`, `/verbose`, `/think`, ...) and plain messages are unaffected; gateway (non-local) mode keeps forwarding every command unchanged.

Fixes #71592

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** In `openclaw tui --local`, advertised shared slash commands with no local TUI handler (`/status`, `/compact`, `/commands`, `/tools`, `/whoami`, `/id`, and the rest of the shared registry) no longer create a user/model turn. They render an explicit "not supported in local mode" system line and are dropped from local autocomplete/help, while normal messages and locally-handled commands still work.

**Real environment tested:** Real `openclaw tui --local` driven in a PTY (`@lydell/node-pty`) via the repo's local TUI smoke lane, with only the external model HTTP endpoint mocked (the real local embedded backend boots and runs). Linux, Node 22. PR head `14b1ba0593627237bb86c2e18bc0dbb74c211195`, rebased on `origin/main` `5bd8dbd0b89a6addad7d6ff1cce0897d4c80a3f6`. Plus focused Vitest unit coverage for the interception, autocomplete, and help-text layers.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
# Real local TUI PTY smoke (only the model endpoint is mocked):
OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_INCLUDE_LOCAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 \
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts \
  src/tui/tui-pty-local.e2e.test.ts --reporter=default --testTimeout=150000

# Focused unit lanes:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/commands.test.ts \
  src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts --reporter=dot
```
The PTY test types `/status`, `/compact`, `/commands` into the live local TUI, asserts the unsupported text appears, asserts `mockModel.requests()` is empty (no `/v1/responses`, no user/model turn), then sends a normal message as a positive control and asserts the real model path still fires.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted visible terminal output captured from the real local TUI PTY session:
```
 local ready | idle
 local embedded TUI mode does not support /status; message not sent
 local embedded TUI mode does not support /compact; message not sent
 local embedded TUI mode does not support /commands; message not sent
 streaming 0s | local ready
LOCAL_PTY_RESPONSE
```

**Observed result after fix:**
```
 ✓ |tui-pty| src/tui/tui-pty-local.e2e.test.ts (2 tests) 228253ms
     ✓ drives the real local backend with a mocked model endpoint  116077ms
     ✓ answers unsupported shared slash commands without a model turn (#71592)  112172ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
```
```
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  136 passed (136)   # src/tui/commands.test.ts + tui-command-handlers.test.ts + tui.test.ts
```
In the PTY run the mock model server received zero requests during the three blocked commands (`mockModel.requests()` empty) and exactly one request for the positive-control message, proving the blocked commands never start a local embedded run.

**What was not tested:** No human-interactive full-screen session and no real cloud provider call (the model endpoint is mocked per the repo's `tui --local` smoke lane); gateway-connected mode was exercised only through unit tests, not a live gateway. Provider auth, streaming against a real model, and non-Linux terminals were not exercised.

## Validation
- `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint` clean on all changed files.
- `tsgo:core` typecheck clean.
- `git diff --check` clean.

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache provider-normalized tool schema `parameters` for repeated embedded agent turns when the active provider hook exposes a stable hook-owned cache key.
- Keep the cache conservative: the key includes provider hook identity plus provider/model/runtime inputs and ordered original tool schema signatures; cached values clone only tool `parameters`, not tool objects, execute closures, approval state, or guards.
- Add `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` as a kill switch, SDK docs/API surface for `resolveToolSchemaCacheKey`, and focused cache-key, clone-safety, env-disable, eviction, and runtime-handle coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated embedded agent turns no longer redo provider tool-schema normalization when the provider hook identity, provider/model/config inputs, and ordered tool schema signature are unchanged. Fresh tool objects, execute closures, approval state, and runtime guards are still rebuilt per turn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, current head `98e63ff4a9e4d5e357b34ae20a07c79d510a09dc`, rebased onto latest `origin/main` at `ec2455a842cfc038fa8551415dcc20d177257ab7`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebased the branch onto current `origin/main`, regenerated the plugin SDK API baseline from the merged tree, ran the focused schema/runtime/doctor tests, checked the generated SDK API baseline, linted the touched TypeScript files, checked markdown/docs indexing, and verified whitespace/conflict markers with `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --write
Wrote docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
Wrote docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.json (gitignored, local only)
Wrote docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.jsonl (gitignored, local only)

node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts
4 Vitest shards passed: 6 files / 123 tests

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugins/provider-hook-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/types.ts
exit 0

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
exit 0

node scripts/docs-list.js
exit 0

git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main HEAD
exit 0
```

Observed result after fix: the provider schema-normalization cache stores normalized parameters once and reuses cloned parameters on subsequent matching calls while preserving fresh tool closures. Regression coverage also proves cache keys are resolved from the same prepared provider runtime handle used for normalization, preventing cross-plugin reuse.

What was not tested: full live-provider end-to-end latency was not tested. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache complete tool inventory/materialization, policy resolution, prompt inputs, or full agent-turn startup.
