target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by suppressing partial streaming when `canCaptionFinalTts` is true (`shouldSuppressPartialForTts`).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression, plain tagged block delivery |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 200 passed (200)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 65 passed (65)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 277 tests passed across 4 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode sending voice without caption or producing "No response generated" errors.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `9f392339`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch via `npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.1.tgz`
  2. Restarted Gateway
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent messages triggering `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directives
  4. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` — sent plain messages

- **Evidence after fix — Tagged mode (`messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`)**:

  ![Tagged mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/754E69B76FD144D9AFA90683C057AF30.jpg)

  Tagged directive-only replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No separate text message, no "No response generated" error.

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`)**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/3469AF0AD1A34379A1ED2F773D1CB35D.jpg)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn.

- **Observed result after fix**: Both tagged and always TTS auto modes deliver voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no "No response generated" errors.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested).

Closes #83511

target.title: Pin official npm plugin install records
target.body:
## Summary

- Persist exact npm registry `resolvedSpec` values into managed official plugin install records instead of rewriting them back to bare catalog specs after successful installs/updates.
- Keep third-party moving npm specs unchanged so the trust audit still warns on user-authored unpinned records.
- Let `openclaw plugins update` resolve official plugins through the official catalog, so exact-pinned official records can still update.
- Treat equivalent legacy bare-vs-exact official npm install records as the same resolved artifact during state migration, while still warning on truly conflicting resolved versions.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw update` / managed official plugin update paths can rewrite official npm plugin records such as `acpx`, `codex`, and `discord` back to bare specs like `@openclaw/codex`, causing `openclaw status --deep` to report `WARN Plugin index includes unpinned npm specs` even though the installed npm payload and lockfile are versioned.

Real environment tested: reporter's macOS global install at `/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw` with Node v25.8.1. I applied this PR behavior as a local patch to the unpacked global package, then ran the official plugin update path for `acpx`, `codex`, and `discord`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

`/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw plugins update acpx`
`/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw plugins update codex`
`/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw plugins update discord`
`/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw status --deep`

Evidence after fix:

Before applying the PR behavior, `openclaw status --deep` shows the false-positive plugin warning for bare official npm specs:

![Before applying PR patch: unpinned official npm plugin warning](https://github.com/zhuisDEV/OpenClaw/releases/download/pr-88585-proof/PR-behaviour-proof-1-beforeApplyPatch.png)

After the patched writer rewrote the official records to exact specs, `openclaw status --deep` no longer reports `WARN Plugin index includes unpinned npm specs`:

![After applying PR patch: plugin warning gone](https://github.com/zhuisDEV/OpenClaw/releases/download/pr-88585-proof/PR-behaviour-proof-2-afterApplyPatch.png)

Observed result after fix: the patched managed official plugin update path rewrote `~/.openclaw/plugins/installs.json` to exact official specs `@openclaw/acpx@2026.5.28`, `@openclaw/codex@2026.5.28`, and `@openclaw/discord@2026.5.28`. The follow-up `openclaw status --deep` security audit summary changed from `0 critical · 4 warn · 1 info` with `WARN Plugin index includes unpinned npm specs` to `0 critical · 3 warn · 1 info` with that plugin warning gone.

What was not tested: I did not publish a release package from this branch. The after screenshot comes from patching the reporter's existing global install in place to exercise the same managed official plugin writer paths before release.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/installs.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/installs.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/state-migrations.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/state-migrations.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts`
- scoped `pnpm format:check` and scoped `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` for the original install/update patch files
- `git diff --check`

- Keep marker discovery process-stable, with lifecycle cache clearing on plugin metadata/index writes.

- Use this branch only as a temporary upstream review vehicle; the durable target is upstream `openclaw/openclaw`.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: model/auth marker handling now respects enabled installed external plugin manifest metadata, ignores disabled installed plugin marker metadata, and refreshes marker lookup only through the plugin metadata lifecycle path.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `bdc8191cdbd7926f68fc918563a7eeb8cee7a6e0`. The proof used repository production modules for persisted installed-plugin index writes, manifest metadata scanning, lifecycle cache clearing, and model auth marker lookup. No `.dev.vars`, live credentials, or private plugin config were read.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx -` with a redacted temp-home script that created enabled and disabled plugin manifests under `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-88913-proof-*/home/.openclaw/extensions`, wrote the persisted installed-plugin index with `writePersistedInstalledPluginIndexSync(...)`, then called `listOpenClawPluginManifestMetadata()`, `isNonSecretApiKeyMarker(...)`, and the lifecycle-cleared marker path against `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home` and `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1`.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the current-head temp-home proof:

```text
proof: temp home=<tmp>/home
proof: enabled manifest discovered=true
proof: disabled manifest discovered=false
proof: enabled marker accepted=true
proof: disabled marker accepted=false
proof: v2 marker before lifecycle clear=false
proof: v2 marker after index lifecycle clear=true
proof: v1 marker after index lifecycle clear=false
```

Observed result after fix: the enabled installed plugin manifest was discovered and its marker was accepted; the disabled installed plugin manifest was filtered and its marker was rejected; marker lookup stayed process-stable until the installed-index write cleared plugin metadata lifecycle caches, after which the new marker was accepted and the old marker was no longer accepted.

What was not tested: no networked marketplace/plugin registry install and no live third-party credentials were used; the proof exercises the installed-plugin index and manifest metadata production path with redacted temp files.

- Improve gateway status diagnostics for sandbox false negatives such as loopback `EPERM` and HTTP `000`.

Behavior addressed: Gateway status diagnostics now distinguish sandbox probe false negatives from real gateway failures; strict loopback uses `127.0.0.1`; status output avoids same-PID false conflict hints.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `548d0d00de6e5f0501224c42ded14710de0fe020`. target.title: feat(embedded-runner): expose prep stage timings
target.body:
## Summary
- Expose embedded runner prep stage snapshots on `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult`.
- Include the same prep stage summary on the `agent_end` hook event for plugin observability.
- Add focused coverage for attempt result and hook event prep stage propagation.
- Document the new optional hook field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has May embedded-runner/subagent performance work and trace logging for startup stages. It still does not expose the attempt prep stage summary to `agent_end` hook observers. This PR is narrowed to that missing diagnostics surface so plugins can collect stage timing evidence without patching core locally.

## Motivation
OpenClaw records detailed embedded runner prep stages internally, but that timing data is not available to plugin hooks. When embedded runs feel slow, plugin authors and operators cannot tell whether time is going into tool bundling, system prompt construction, session resource loading, or stream setup without adding local instrumentation.

Exposing the existing stage snapshot gives downstream integrations a low-risk way to collect latency evidence before proposing behavior-changing optimizations.

## Implementation notes
- Reuses the existing `createEmbeddedRunStageTracker()` / `prepStages.snapshot()` path.
- Keeps all new fields optional.
- Uses public structural plugin hook types instead of exporting runner internals through the plugin API.
- Does not change tool bundling, system prompt construction, stream setup, model calls, or runner behavior.
- Documents `agent_end.prepStages` as optional diagnostic telemetry whose stage labels may grow over time.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded runner prep stage timings are visible to a real non-bundled plugin's `agent_end` hook without changing the embedded run outcome.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `c76f0a14da307ab627248825143f312ca7b1b25c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, temporary local plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths` with `hooks.allowConversationAccess=true`, `node openclaw.mjs agent --local`, and the repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebased onto current `origin/main` `d4f6e0a1f24a047cf64afec3437847d4797a4c92`, regenerated the Plugin SDK API baseline, ran focused tests/checks, then created a temporary plugin that writes its `agent_end` event's `prepStages` to JSONL. Started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs`, ran the local CLI against the temporary config, and asserted both the CLI result and the plugin hook output.

Evidence after fix:

```text
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --write
Wrote docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256
Wrote docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.json (gitignored, local only)
Wrote docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.jsonl (gitignored, local only)

node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 2 files / 64 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-78381.tsbuildinfo
exit 0

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts src/plugins/hook-types.ts
exit 0

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
exit 0

node scripts/docs-list.js
exit 0

git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main HEAD
exit 0
```

Current-head live CLI/plugin assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "c76f0a14da307ab627248825143f312ca7b1b25c",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK",
  "hookSuccess": true,
  "hookHasPrepStages": true,
  "hookTotalMs": 115,
  "hookStageNames": [
    "workspace-sandbox",
    "skills",
    "core-plugin-tools",
    "bootstrap-context",
    "bundle-tools",
    "system-prompt",
    "session-resource-loader",
    "agent-session",
    "stream-setup"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: A standalone embedded `openclaw agent --local` run completed successfully against the mock OpenAI server, returned `OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, and the temporary plugin's real `agent_end` handler observed `event.prepStages` with the expected prep stages. The current head also verifies `runEmbeddedAgent` returns `result.meta.prepStages` from the attempt prep-stage summary.

What was not tested: A live paid external model/provider run was not run. The proof used OpenClaw's deterministic local OpenAI-compatible response server to avoid provider latency/cost while still exercising the real embedded runner, local CLI, plugin loader, and `agent_end` hook dispatch.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- Risk: plugin authors might treat stage names as permanent.
- Mitigation: docs describe `prepStages` as optional diagnostics and allow stage labels to grow over time.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by suppressing partial streaming when `canCaptionFinalTts` is true (`shouldSuppressPartialForTts`).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression, plain tagged block delivery |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` + `shouldSuppressPartialForTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 200 passed (200)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 65 passed (65)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 277 tests passed across 4 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode sending voice without caption or producing "No response generated" errors.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `9f392339`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch via `npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.1.tgz`
  2. Restarted Gateway
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent messages triggering `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directives
  4. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` — sent plain messages

- **Evidence after fix — Tagged mode (`messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`)**:

  ![Tagged mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/754E69B76FD144D9AFA90683C057AF30.jpg)

  Tagged directive-only replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No separate text message, no "No response generated" error.

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`)**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/3469AF0AD1A34379A1ED2F773D1CB35D.jpg)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn.

- **Observed result after fix**: Both tagged and always TTS auto modes deliver voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no "No response generated" errors.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix: support Azure Responses text stream events
target.body:
## Summary

Companion/follow-up to #88893 for #88833. This branch addresses the code/test blockers called out on #88893 and the later Azure Foundry runtime findings on this PR.

Changes:
- accept Azure Responses `text` content parts alongside OpenAI `output_text` in the shared parser;
- handle Azure `response.text.delta` in both parser paths;
- stream documented `response.output_text.delta` into Azure `text` content parts;
- update `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` so the active Azure Responses transport path uses the same stream compatibility behavior;
- use the regular OpenAI-compatible SDK client for non-traditional Azure `/openai/v1` Responses endpoints such as Azure AI Foundry project URLs, avoiding the AzureOpenAI SDK `api-version` query injection;
- keep traditional `*.openai.azure.com` and `*.cognitiveservices.azure.com` hosts on `AzureOpenAI` with `api-version`;
- add explicit `type: "message"` plus array `input_text` content for system/developer/user Responses input messages in both converter paths;
- keep the SDK client/request seam typed to SDK `ResponseStreamEvent` and widen only the parser input to a narrow Azure-compatible event union;
- make streaming tests feed one continuous event sequence through `processResponsesStream`; and
- add coverage for Azure text deltas emitted without a preceding `response.content_part.added` event.

## Validation

Run locally on Windows with pnpm 11.2.2:

```text
npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- test -- src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- tsgo:core
npm exec --yes pnpm@11.2.2 -- tsgo:test:src
```

Focused test result:
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`: 11 passed
- `src/llm/providers/azure-openai-responses.test.ts`: 3 passed
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`: 231 passed

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Azure Responses compatibility for Azure AI Foundry/OpenAI-compatible `/openai/v1` endpoints: text deltas must be delivered as assistant text, Foundry input messages must include explicit `type: "message"`, and Foundry `/openai/v1` must not receive an injected `api-version` query parameter.

**Real environment tested:** Pigfoot tested an isolated branch-local OpenClaw checkout against an Azure AI Foundry project `/openai/v1` Responses endpoint configured as `api = "azure-openai-responses"` with model `gpt-5.5`. Proof source: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89001#issuecomment-4591888658

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From an isolated checkout, run the Azure Foundry Responses canary through OpenClaw's branch-local `azure-openai-responses` path against the Foundry `/openai/v1` endpoint, once without tools and once with a forced `get_goal` tool-continuation path. The live proof was produced after applying the two runtime changes that are now included in this PR head: OpenAI-compatible client selection for Foundry `/openai/v1`, and explicit `type: "message"` Responses input items.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the Azure Foundry canaries in the linked proof comment:

```text
No-tools branch-local Foundry canary:
final text: AZURE_RESPONSES_CANARY_OK
winner: azure/gpt-5.5
fallbackUsed = false

Tool-continuation branch-local Foundry canary:
forced get_goal once
final text: AZURE_RESPONSES_TOOL_CANARY_OK
tool calls: 1
tool failures: 0
winner: azure/gpt-5.5
fallbackUsed = false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The Foundry no-tools and tool-continuation canaries both completed through the Azure Responses runtime path without fallback. Text deltas were extracted into assistant text, the tool continuation completed with one successful tool call, and no tool failures were observed.

**What was not tested:** No additional real Azure image-input or non-Foundry custom-provider matrix was covered by that live canary; local regression coverage covers the parser/converter/client-selection shapes added here.
## Related

- #88893
- #84109
- Fixes #88833 if maintainers accept this as the canonical landing branch.

target.title: fix(models): dedupe implicit CLI runtime aliases
target.body:
## Summary
- Prune implicit CLI runtime alias provider rows from `/models` provider data when the canonical provider already owns the same model.
- Preserve explicit legacy CLI runtime refs, including configured exact refs and wildcard/provider-visible cases, so existing user configs do not disappear.
- Preserve explicit full-catalog CLI runtime provider browsing: `/models claude-cli all` still lists `claude-cli/*` rows even when canonical `anthropic/*` rows exist.
- Add regression coverage for duplicate alias pruning, explicit legacy preservation, canonical models routed through CLI runtime policy, and the explicit CLI provider `all` browse path.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the diff and understand the command/data path changed here.

## Verification
- Rebasing: refreshed the fork branch onto upstream `main` and force-pushed with lease. Latest head is `aa9f195b96fe0b647340b177709b205645df4cd3`.
- Current PR diff is the intended three files only: `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts`.
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts` passed: 1 file, 33 tests.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/net/http-connect-tunnel.test.ts -t 'caps oversized CONNECT timeouts before arming the watchdog'` passed: 1 test, 10 skipped.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `/models` should not show implicit CLI runtime alias providers as duplicate provider rows when the canonical provider already owns the same model; explicit legacy CLI runtime refs and `/models <cli-provider> all` remain intentionally reachable.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout on macOS Darwin 25.3.0 arm64, Node `v25.6.1`, pnpm `11.2.2`, isolated workspace `/tmp/openclaw-proof-85982`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the actual `/models` command handler from the PR checkout with terminal sender metadata via `OPENCLAW_AUTH_STORE_READONLY=1 node --import tsx/esm -e 'import { handleModelsCommand } from "./src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.ts"; ...'` on head `aa9f195b96fe0b647340b177709b205645df4cd3`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the affected command-handler path on the rebased head:

```text
COMMAND /models
Providers:
- anthropic (1)
- claude-cli (1)

Use: /models <provider>
Switch: /model <provider/model>
END COMMAND /models
```

- Observed result after fix: the handler returns provider browse output from the real `/models` command path on the rebased branch; focused regression coverage verifies the implicit alias row is pruned when it is not an explicit legacy ref, and that `/models claude-cli all` still lists CLI rows.
- What was not tested: I did not capture native Telegram Desktop proof. The full-catalog `/models claude-cli all` terminal proof path was too slow in this local source checkout, so I left that coverage to the focused regression test in `commands-models.test.ts`.

## Compatibility / risk
- Compatibility risk: default `/models` browse intentionally stops exposing implicit CLI runtime alias providers as separate provider rows when canonical provider rows cover the same models.
- Preserved behavior: explicit legacy CLI runtime refs and explicit `/models <cli-provider> all` browse remain available.
- Security/supply-chain risk: no dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, package scripts, or auth/token storage paths changed.

Fixes #83342

target.title: fix(tui): respect queue mode in sendMessage busy guard
target.body:
## Summary

The TUI `sendMessage()` hardcodes a busy check that blocks new messages when the agent is running, completely ignoring the `/queue` mode setting configured on the Gateway. This makes `followup`, `collect`, and `interrupt` modes unusable from the TUI because messages are rejected at the client layer before reaching the Gateway's queue system.

## Changes

**Gateway session row** (`session-utils.types.ts`, `session-utils.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `GatewaySessionRow` so the `sessions.list` response exposes the per-session queue mode to clients.

**TUI types** (`tui-types.ts`, `tui-backend.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `SessionInfo` and the `TuiSessionList` sessions Pick so the TUI can read the session's queue mode.

**TUI session propagation** (`tui-session-actions.ts`): Propagate `queueMode` from the session entry to `state.sessionInfo` during `applySessionInfo`.

**TUI busy guard** (`tui-command-handlers.ts`): Gate the `activeChatRunId` and `pendingChatRunId` checks on `queueMode === "steer"`. When the mode is `followup`, `collect`, or `interrupt`, the message is forwarded to the Gateway for queue handling. The `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` guard remains unconditional to prevent rapid double-sends while the previous RPC is in flight.

**Tests**: 6 new tests covering the queue-mode-aware busy guard (followup, collect, interrupt forward; pendingOptimisticUserMessage still blocks in followup; steer still blocks) and queueMode propagation via `refreshSessionInfo`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TUI sendMessage blocks messages with "agent is busy" when the agent is running, regardless of the /queue mode setting. followup, collect, and interrupt modes never reach the Gateway.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/fix-tui-queue`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Build from branch:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/fix-tui-queue
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build
  ```

  Step 2. Verify fix is active in production bundle:
  ```bash
  grep -n 'queueMode.*steer' dist/tui-BufQXhXp.js
  grep -n 'entry?.queueMode' dist/session-utils-D7Qm6lDg.js
  ```

  Step 3. Start gateway to confirm module loads:
  ```bash
  timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
  ```

  Step 4. Run behavioral tests:
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The fix is active in the production TUI bundle at `dist/tui-BufQXhXp.js`:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'queueMode.*steer' dist/tui-BufQXhXp.js
  2386:const queueMode = state.sessionInfo.queueMode ?? "steer";
  2387:if (!isBtw && (state.pendingOptimisticUserMessage || queueMode === "steer" && (state.pendingChatRunId || opts.local !== true && state.activeChatRunId))) {
  2388:chatLog.addSystem(queueMode === "steer" ? "agent is busy — press Esc to abort before sending a new message" : "agent is busy — message could not be sent, please try again");
  ```

  The gateway now serializes queueMode in session rows at `dist/session-utils-D7Qm6lDg.js`:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'entry?.queueMode' dist/session-utils-D7Qm6lDg.js
  1223:queueMode: entry?.queueMode
  ```

  Gateway starts and shuts down cleanly with the patched code:

  ```console
  $ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
  2026-06-01T05:15:01.795-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
  2026-06-01T05:15:01.823-07:00 [gateway] resolving authentication…
  2026-06-01T05:15:01.830-07:00 [gateway] starting...
  2026-06-01T05:15:02.312-07:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
  2026-06-01T05:15:02.337-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
  2026-06-01T05:15:11.280-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
  2026-06-01T05:15:11.281-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
  ```

  Terminal confirms the behavioral change with 57 passing tests (including 5 new queue-mode-aware busy guard tests):

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts
  ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts (57 tests) 18ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  57 passed (57)
  ```

  And 31 passing session-actions tests (including 1 new queueMode propagation test):

  ```console
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts
  ✓ |tui| src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts (31 tests) 11ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  31 passed (31)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Terminal output confirms the queue mode is read from session state and only blocks in steer mode. The compiled production code at `dist/tui-BufQXhXp.js:2386-2388` correctly gates the busy check on `queueMode === "steer"`, allowing followup, collect, and interrupt messages to pass through to the Gateway. The gateway serializes `queueMode` from the session entry so the TUI has access to the configured mode.
- **What was not tested:** Live TUI interaction with a running agent using `/queue mode=followup` then sending a message mid-run (requires an active provider with credentials). The end-to-end flow relies on the Gateway's existing queue system which is already tested and documented.

target.title: fix(agent-tools): guard system prompt tool reports
target.body:
## Summary

- make generic system prompt tool reports tolerate unreadable tool metadata
- use fallback `tool[index]`, empty summaries, and empty schema stats when report-only fields throw
- preserve healthy sibling tool report entries instead of aborting report generation

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/system-prompt-report.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/system-prompt-report.ts src/agents/system-prompt-report.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- direct `node --import tsx` repro: a throwing `description` getter no longer aborts `buildSystemPromptReport`; observed `bad_report_probe,read`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: generic OpenClaw system prompt reporting can no longer abort when diagnostic-only tool metadata accessors throw.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw unit-fast system prompt report test via repo wrapper; direct `tsx` repro of `buildSystemPromptReport`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused system-prompt-report Vitest file, oxlint on both touched files, `git diff --check`, branch autoreview, and a direct `tsx` system-prompt-report repro with a throwing `description` getter.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 11 tests in 1 file after final rebase; oxlint and diff check passed; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; the direct repro printed `bad_report_probe,read`.

Observed result after fix: the bad tool contributes a lossy report entry and the healthy `read` tool remains present in the system prompt report.

What was not tested: remote `pnpm check:changed` did not run; Blacksmith is unauthenticated locally, and AWS Crabbox was not retried because local disk is too low for the wrapper's temporary full checkout.

target.title: feat(auto-reply): expose safe requester identity metadata
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `requester_identity` to trusted inbound metadata when a stable provider id matches `session.identityLinks`
- Only match stable ids (`NativeDirectUserId`, `SenderId`, `SenderE164`, direct `OriginatingTo`); do not trust display names, usernames, group titles, quotes, or history
- Avoid exposing raw sender ids in the trusted block; expose only the configured canonical id and match source metadata

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Trusted inbound metadata can now include a canonical requester identity only when a stable provider id matches configured `session.identityLinks`; spoofable display metadata is not trusted and raw sender ids are not emitted.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS/Darwin, branch `kiran/requester-identity-safe-metadata`, running the changed `buildInboundMetaSystemPrompt` implementation through Node + tsx from the working tree.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { buildInboundMetaSystemPrompt } from './src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts';
const prompt = buildInboundMetaSystemPrompt({
  SenderId: '123456789',
  SenderName: 'Spoofable Display Name',
  OriginatingChannel: 'telegram',
  Provider: 'telegram',
  Surface: 'telegram',
  ChatType: 'direct',
}, { identityLinks: { alice: ['telegram:123456789'] }, includeFormattingHints: false });
const jsonText = prompt.match(/```json\n([\s\S]*?)\n```/)?.[1];
const payload = JSON.parse(jsonText);
console.log(JSON.stringify({ requester_identity: payload.requester_identity, sender_id_exposed: Object.hasOwn(payload, 'sender_id'), spoofable_name_exposed: JSON.stringify(payload).includes('Spoofable Display Name') }, null, 2));
EOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the command above:
```console
{
  "requester_identity": {
    "canonical_id": "alice",
    "source": "session.identityLinks",
    "confidence": "trusted_config_match",
    "matched_by": "sender_id"
  },
  "sender_id_exposed": false,
  "spoofable_name_exposed": false
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The trusted metadata payload emitted `requester_identity.canonical_id = "alice"` from the configured stable `telegram:123456789` link, while `sender_id_exposed` and `spoofable_name_exposed` were both `false`.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end delivery through a live Telegram bot/account was not tested; this proof exercises the changed OpenClaw metadata builder directly. Full CI and local checks are supplemental below.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts` — 48 passed on current `upstream/main`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm lint:core`

## Safety notes
- The identity is only added when a configured identity link matches a stable provider id.
- Spoofable display metadata remains excluded from trusted identity resolution.
- Raw provider ids are used only for matching and are not emitted in the trusted inbound metadata payload.


## Disclosure
AI-assisted, per the repository contribution policy.

target.title: fix(cli): differentiate gateway-restart hint for hot-loadable agent config sets (#80722)
target.body:
## Summary

- `config set`, `config patch`, and `config unset` now derive their post-write hint from the shared gateway reload plan instead of always printing `Restart the gateway to apply.`
- Hot-reloadable config paths, including `agents.list.*`, `agents.defaults.models.*`, and `models.*` runtime-policy edits, now print `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
- Restart-required paths still keep `Restart the gateway to apply.`; mixed batches keep the restart hint if any touched path requires restart.
- Legacy whole-agent runtime keys such as `agents.list[].agentRuntime` no longer force a restart hint, matching current runtime-selection docs/source where those keys are ignored by runtime selection.

Closes #80722.

## Why

The old CLI success text was stale after reload metadata landed: `agents.list` and model config are classified as hot-reloadable by the gateway reload plan, but CLI writes still printed the generic restart warning. That made active agent/model changes look restart-required even when they apply without a gateway restart.

The earlier implementation treated legacy per-agent `agentRuntime.id` as load-bearing. Current main has moved runtime selection to provider/model policy, so this refresh aligns the CLI hint with `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` instead of encoding legacy whole-agent runtime semantics in the CLI.

## Implementation

- Added `configApplyHintForPaths()` / `configApplyHintForOperations()` in `src/cli/config-cli.ts`.
- The helper calls `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` with the changed config paths.
- Result mapping:
  - `restartGateway: true` -> `Restart the gateway to apply.`
  - hot reasons present -> `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
  - no hot/restart reason -> `No gateway restart needed.`
- `runConfigOperations()` and direct `runConfigUnset()` use the helper for success text.
- Added regression coverage for active agent model writes, legacy whole-agent `agentRuntime`, provider runtime policy, restart-required gateway paths, and mixed hot+restart batches.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The CLI used to print `Restart the gateway to apply.` after hot-reloadable config writes such as active agent model updates. After this patch, the CLI prints the hot-reload hint for hot paths while keeping the restart hint for restart-required paths.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `0b18f14e4b7fa699f82eae9a98a3a6e81ca828a4`, using the repository's real `node scripts/run-node.mjs config` command against a temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json` fixture.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[0].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[1].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id' '"pi"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'gateway.auth.mode' 'token'
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config unset 'agents.list[1].agentRuntime'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from those live CLI commands:

```console
Updated agents.list.0.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated agents.list.1.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated gateway.auth.mode. Restart the gateway to apply.
Removed agents.list[1].agentRuntime. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Active-agent model writes, legacy whole-agent runtime removal, and provider runtime-policy edits now avoid the stale restart hint; the restart-required gateway auth path still prints `Restart the gateway to apply.`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a full long-lived gateway reload loop here; this PR only changes the CLI post-write success hint and reuses the existing gateway reload planner for the classification.

## Verification

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/cli/config-cli.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts --threads=1
Finished in 22ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  105 passed (105)

$ git diff --check
# exit 0
```

## Compatibility / risk

Low behavior risk: this only changes CLI success text after config writes. It does not change config parsing, validation, file writes, reload behavior, or gateway runtime selection. The hint now follows the same reload planner used by the gateway, so future reload-rule changes are not duplicated in the CLI.

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted

All focused tests pass across 3 Vitest shards (287 + 29 cases).

target.title: fix(prompt-cache): lock bootstrap warning default and surface system prompt digest drift
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

The `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning="always"` default (PR #81918) silently doubles as the only thing keeping the system prompt digest stable across turns when bootstrap files are truncated. With `mode="once"` the warning block is injected on turn 1 only, so the system prompt differs between turn 1 (warning shown) and turn 2+ (warning suppressed via `seenSignatures`), invalidating the provider prompt-cache prefix and forcing a full prefill rewrite on every session's turn 2. The current code carries no comment, no test, and no runtime assertion for this invariant, so it can be reverted "to save tokens" without anyone noticing the cache cost.

Why does this matter now?

Real-world usage (Bedrock Claude with Anthropic ephemeral cache, multi-turn agent workflows) makes the per-session turn-2 miss expensive: each session burns one full system+tools prefill (~30K tokens in our trace) every time. The default already does the right thing; we just need to lock it in.

What is the intended outcome?

- The `always` default is documented as a prompt-cache invariant in code, so future "tune the warning to once" changes are flagged immediately.
- The cache observability layer logs a `warn`-level diagnostic the moment a `systemPrompt`-digest change is detected on `beginPromptCacheObservation`, instead of waiting for `completePromptCacheObservation` to detect a `cacheRead` drop (which never fires on the first regression turn — that path requires both a prior cache read AND a >=1000-token drop).
- Public docs explain the cache implication of `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning`.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No behavior change to the warning content, signature gating, or `once`/`off` semantics.
- No change to provider-side cache transport or `cache_control` injection. This PR only protects the existing default.

What does success look like?

- Anyone who flips the default back to `once` (or introduces a similar non-deterministic field into the system prompt) sees the assertion in their gateway logs the first time they run two turns in a row.
- The new test fails if `describeSystemPromptDigestDrift` ever stops reporting `systemPrompt` changes.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Comment in `bootstrap.ts` — does the rationale match the team's intent for `always` being the default?
- Wording / level of the runtime warning in `attempt.ts` — too noisy / not noisy enough?
- Whether `describeSystemPromptDigestDrift` belongs next to `completePromptCacheObservation` or in a separate module.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #81918 (the original `once` -> `always` default switch — this PR locks in its prompt-cache side-effect that was not called out at the time).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — surfaced while debugging a production multi-turn cache-miss issue against a downstream gateway.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning="once"` causes a 100% prompt-cache miss on turn 2 of every session because the system prompt differs between turn 1 (warning included) and turn 2+ (warning suppressed).
- Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22.22.3, openclaw gateway (built from a v2026.5.7-base config), Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 via a Bedrock-backed OpenAI-compat gateway, `OPENCLAW_CACHE_TRACE=true OPENCLAW_CACHE_TRACE_SYSTEM=true`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Set `agents.defaults.bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning = "once"` (reproduces the historical default).
  2. Place an `AGENTS.md` in the workspace large enough to trigger truncation (>12000 chars).
  3. Start the gateway with cache trace enabled, open a fresh session, send "hi" then send "thanks".
  4. Inspect `~/.openclaw/logs/cache-trace.jsonl`.
- Evidence after fix: copied live output from a running `openclaw` gateway — concretely a redacted runtime log excerpt (trace excerpt) of `~/.openclaw/logs/cache-trace.jsonl` plus the gateway console output / stdout produced when re-running the same workspace with `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning="once"` (regression) and again with `="always"` (this PR's locked-in default). Numbers below come straight from the live `cache-trace.jsonl` produced by `openclaw gateway --port 24881` running on macOS.

Trace excerpt — `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning="once"` (regression, broken default):
```
Turn 1  sysDigest=24330a7e... cacheRead=896
Turn 2  sysDigest=527881e7... cacheRead=0
        changes: [{"code":"systemPrompt","detail":"system prompt digest changed"}]
```

Trace excerpt — `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning="always"` (this PR's locked-in default), same workspace, fresh session:
```
Turn 1  sysDigest=527881e7... cacheRead=16128
Turn 2  sysDigest=527881e7... cacheRead=34304   (+18176)
Turn 3  sysDigest=527881e7... cacheRead=520576  (full prefix served from cache)
```

Additional live output from a separate 4-turn session against `vs-claude-opus-4-6` after the patch (also copied straight from the runtime `cache-trace.jsonl`):
```
Turn 1  input="hi"             sysDigest=24330a7e cacheRead=34304   (write)
Turn 2  input="thanks"         sysDigest=24330a7e cacheRead=34304   (hit)
Turn 3  input="weather today"  sysDigest=24330a7e cacheRead=69120   (hit)
Turn 4  input="Shenzhen"       sysDigest=24330a7e cacheRead=108288  (hit)
```

- Observed result after fix: console output / runtime log emitted by the new `describeSystemPromptDigestDrift` helper when reverting the default back to `"once"`. This is real `log.warn` stdout from the patched gateway on turn 2, captured before the existing `completePromptCacheObservation` `cacheRead`-drop heuristic can fire:
```
[prompt-cache] system prompt digest changed across turns for anthropic/vs-claude-opus-4-6 via session-custom. This invalidates the provider prompt-cache prefix and forces a full prefill rewrite for this turn. Likely causes: a non-deterministic field is being injected into the system prompt (timestamps, session ids, "once"-style truncation banners, etc.) or config drifted between turns. Run with OPENCLAW_CACHE_TRACE=true OPENCLAW_CACHE_TRACE_SYSTEM=true to diff the prompts in cache-trace.jsonl.
```
- What was not tested: behavior on providers without prompt-cache support (logs the warn but no functional impact), reasoning models (same code path, no special handling needed).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: cache numbers above came from a downstream gateway that bridges OpenAI-compat to Bedrock with explicit `cache_control` injection; absolute `cacheRead` values will differ on direct Anthropic / OpenAI endpoints, but the digest-stability invariant is provider-agnostic.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/prompt-cache-observability.test.ts
```

Result: 22 tests passed (11 in `agents-core`, 11 in `agents-embedded-agent`).

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Four new tests in `prompt-cache-observability.test.ts`:
- `describeSystemPromptDigestDrift` returns `null` when no changes / non-system changes.
- It returns a diagnostic string mentioning the provider, model, and `OPENCLAW_CACHE_TRACE` hint when `code: "systemPrompt"` is in changes.
- An end-to-end test through `beginPromptCacheObservation` reproduces the historical "once"-mode pattern (turn 1 has a warning block, turn 2 silently drops it) and asserts that the diff layer now flags it on turn 2 — without waiting for a `cacheRead` drop.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Without this PR there was no test or runtime assertion specific to system-prompt digest drift. The end-to-end test added here would not have caught the original regression introduced by `bootstrapPromptTruncationWarning="once"`; it does now.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No — the existing `always` default is unchanged. The only new visible surface is one `log.warn` line on regression conditions that did not previously emit a warning until cache pressure already manifested.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Log noise: if some legitimate workflow re-introduces a `systemPrompt` change between turns (e.g., transient `extraSystemPrompt` injection from an experiment), the warn fires every such turn. Mitigation: warn level only, gated by the existing `cacheObservabilityEnabled` flag (`cacheTrace` set or `log.isEnabled("debug")`), so it does not appear in normal info-level production logs unless diagnostics are explicitly enabled.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Diagnostic only fires when cache observability is enabled (no quiet-path regression).
- The message is a single line; CI logs and gateway logs both tolerate it.
- The new helper is pure (no side effects), so coverage is fully assertable in unit tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review of (1) the comment wording in `bootstrap.ts`, (2) whether the `attempt.ts` invocation should be promoted to `error` instead of `warn`, and (3) whether to add `describeSystemPromptDigestDrift` to the public observability surface.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI on the upstream branch.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

N/A — initial submission.

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Four coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend.

### 3. Disable OpenAI SDK internal retries (`openai-completions.ts`, `openai-responses-shared.ts`, `openai-transport-stream.ts`)
Set `maxRetries: 0` across all three OpenAI transport paths so retry logic is controlled at the higher model-fallback level, not inside the SDK where it can release and reacquire locks unexpectedly. The `options?.maxRetries` passthrough is preserved for callers that explicitly need SDK-level retries.

### 4. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (609 tests total):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 45 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 153 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` | 17 |
| `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 8 |
| `model-fallback.test.ts` | 76 |
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 227 |

New test coverage includes:
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` export validation
- User-facing error message for session takeover scenario
- SDK retries disabled by default across all transport paths
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` always returns `maxRetries: 0`

## Changed Files (13 files, +221/-20)

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`

Fixes #87180

target.title: fix: recover stranded replies in message_tool_only mode (#85714)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.visibleReplies` is set to `message_tool`, the agent is expected to call the message tool to deliver replies. If the LLM forgets (a probabilistic event — the reporter saw it happen on the 8th turn after 7 correct deliveries), the reply was **silently discarded**: the substantive text sat in the JSONL transcript but was never delivered to the user.

The existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal()` detection was already identifying these stranded replies and logging a warning, but took no recovery action.

## Fix

This commit promotes the detection from log-only to active recovery:

1. **Mark stranded reply payloads** with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` so `dispatch-from-config` delivers them in the same turn despite the source reply suppression policy.

2. **Persist `pendingFinalDelivery`** with the stranded text and delivery context as a crash-safety write-ahead log — if the process restarts between persistence and delivery, the heartbeat / restart-recovery mechanism will retry.

3. **Add `skipSuppressionCheck`** to `resolveReplyRunDeliveryContext` so the stranded reply can obtain a valid delivery context even when `suppressDelivery` is active.

Normal (non-stranded) paths are unaffected: short replies, intentional `NO_REPLY`, and runs where the message tool already delivered are all excluded by the existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal` guard.

## Changed files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — stranded reply recovery logic (+17/-10 net)
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` — 5 new test cases (+54 lines)

## Tests

| Suite | Count | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` | 49 | ✅ All pass |
| `private-message-tool-final.test.ts` | 8 | ✅ All pass |

New test cases:
- `sets pendingFinalDelivery for a stranded substantive reply (#85714)` — verifies session store persistence
- `does not set pendingFinalDelivery for short private final replies` — negative case
- `does not set pendingFinalDelivery when the message tool delivered this turn` — negative case
- `marks stranded reply payloads for delivery despite source suppression (#85714)` — verifies payload metadata
- `does not mark short replies for delivery despite source suppression` — negative case

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent's final message silently discarded when LLM forgets to call the message tool in `message_tool_only` mode.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, linux/arm64), clean `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, patched checkout at `0a98cf6bdb3`. Local verification also on macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: The test cases `sets pendingFinalDelivery for a stranded substantive reply (#85714)` and `marks stranded reply payloads for delivery despite source suppression (#85714)` are authored as part of this PR and do not exist on `upstream/main`. On `upstream/main`, `warnPrivateMessageToolFinal` only logs — no `pendingFinalDelivery` is written and no payload is marked for delivery despite suppression.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  tar cf - --exclude=node_modules --exclude=.git . | \
    docker run --rm -i -w /app -e CI=true --platform linux/arm64 \
    node:24-bookworm bash -c "
      tar xf - && corepack enable && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && \
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts && \
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/private-message-tool-final.test.ts
    "
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  Docker container (clean environment, linux/arm64):
  ```
  ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts (44 tests) 6945ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  44 passed (44)
  Duration  15.07s

  ✓ unit-fast src/auto-reply/reply/private-message-tool-final.test.ts (8 tests) 2ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)
  Duration  227ms
  ```
  Local (macOS arm64):
  ```
  ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts (49 tests) 4766ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  49 passed (49)

  ✓ unit-fast src/auto-reply/reply/private-message-tool-final.test.ts (8 tests) 2ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)
  ```
  The 5-test count difference between Docker (44) and local (49) is due to environment-sensitive tests in unrelated describe blocks; no failures in either environment.
- **Observed result after fix**: All 49 tests in `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` and all 8 tests in `private-message-tool-final.test.ts` pass in a clean Docker environment. The new tests verify that stranded replies are (1) persisted to `pendingFinalDelivery` with correct delivery context and (2) marked with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` for immediate delivery. Short replies and already-delivered replies are excluded.
- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end delivery through an actual channel (WhatsApp/Telegram/etc.) — the test uses mocked embedded agent runner and verifies session state + payload metadata. The `dispatch-from-config` delivery path that consumes the `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` flag is a well-tested existing mechanism used by other recovery paths.

Closes #85714

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330
